verses.commentary: Looking up, he saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury (gazophylakion, γαζοφυλάκιον). The wealthy in their display-phase.
Saw a poor widow putting in two small copper coins (lepta dyo, λεπτὰ δύο — smallest coins in circulation). Her donation is visible only to him.
'Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them.'

They all out of their abundance — she out of her poverty put in all the living she had (panta ton bion, πάντα τὸν βίον — entire livelihood). The math of giving inverted: cost-to-giver, not amount-given.
Some spoke of the temple — adorned with noble stones and offerings. The Herodian temple was an architectural marvel.
'As for these things — the days will come when not one stone will be left upon another which will not be thrown down.' Same prediction as Matthew 24:2.
'Teacher, when will these things be? What sign?' Disciples' question.
'See that you are not led astray. Many will come in my name, saying, I am he, and the time is at hand. Do not follow them.' Eschatological caution.
When you hear of wars and tumults — do not be terrified — these must take place first — the end will not be at once. Sequence matters.
Nation rises against nation, kingdom against kingdom.
Great earthquakes, famines, pestilences (Lukan addition) in various places — terrors and great signs from heaven.
Before all this — they will lay hands on you, persecute you, delivering you to synagogues and prisons, brought before kings and governors for my name's sake.
This will be your opportunity to bear witness (eis martyrion, εἰς μαρτύριον). Persecution is evangelistic platform.
'Settle in your minds not to meditate beforehand how to answer.' Pre-rehearsing defense is unnecessary.
'I will give you mouth and wisdom which none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict.' Jesus himself supplies the words.
Delivered up by parents, brothers, relatives, friends — some of you will be put to death. Personal betrayal predicted.
Hated by all for my name's sake. Universal hostility, particular cause.
'Not a hair of your head will perish.' Paradox alongside martyrdom-prediction: physical death is real, but ultimate preservation is guaranteed.
By your endurance (hypomone, ὑπομονή) you will gain your lives (psychas, ψυχὰς — souls/lives). Perseverance saves.
When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies — know that its desolation has come near. Lukan specificity — direct reference to AD 70 siege (Matthew has the more cryptic 'abomination of desolation').
Then let those in Judea flee to the mountains; those inside the city depart; those in the country not enter the city. Flight instructions.
These are days of vengeance (ekdikeseos, ἐκδικήσεως) — to fulfill all that is written. Prophetic fulfillment, not random calamity.
Woe to pregnant and nursing women in those days. Great distress upon the land, wrath on this people.

Fall by the sword, led captive among all nations; Jerusalem will be trodden by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles (kairoi ethnon, καιροὶ ἐθνῶν) are fulfilled. Lukan unique — pause for Gentile age. AD 70 to Parousia framed as Gentile-times.
Signs in sun, moon, stars. On the earth, distress of nations in perplexity at the roaring of the sea. Cosmic chaos imagery (Isaiah 13; Joel 2).
People fainting with fear, foreboding of what is coming on the world — the powers of the heavens will be shaken. Eschatological dread.
Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory. Daniel 7:13 fulfillment.
When these things begin — straighten up, raise your heads, your redemption (apolytrosis, ἀπολύτρωσις) is drawing near. Eschatological dread reframed as approach of redemption for the faithful.
Parable: see the fig tree and all the trees.
When they put out leaves — you see for yourselves and know summer is near.
So also, when you see these things — know the kingdom of God is near. Read the seasons of history.
'This generation will not pass away until all has taken place.' Disputed reference — Jerusalem's fall, or final generation, or Jewish people. The verb 'all' carries weight either way.
Heaven and earth will pass away — my words will not pass away. Eternal stability of Jesus' speech.
Watch yourselves lest your hearts be weighed down with dissipation (kraipale, κραιπάλη — drunken aftermath), drunkenness, cares of life — and that day come on you suddenly like a trap.
It will come on all who dwell on the face of the whole earth. Universal scope.
Stay awake at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are about to take place, to stand before the Son of Man. Watchfulness and prayer together.
Every day teaching in the temple. At night going out to lodge on the Mount of Olives. Daily rhythm in the last week.
All the people came early in the morning to hear him in the temple. Popular hunger persists even as opposition tightens.
